mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150k persons signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and disposable income, driving equity valuations higher over the subsequent 20 trading days through elevated earnings expectations.
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with sub-5% unemployment target
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following months with PAYEMS > 150k do not exceed baseline returns by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate crossing below 0.25% signals the zero lower bound constraint is binding, indicating the Fed has exhausted conventional policy tools and markets price in extended monetary accommodation, compressing equity risk premiums and supporting forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over the 2012-2024 period.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer spending, which triggers equity market repricing downward as forward earnings forecasts are revised lower over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month
regime_hint: demand-constrained expansion with housing sector weakness
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 when both conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 0.5% month-over-month signals strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, supporting equity valuations through improved corporate profitability over the 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO > 0.5% growth do not exceed baseline by 40 basis points (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and increased recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce risk exposure and produce negative 60-day forward returns.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances following UNRATE spikes > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) declining below 2% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS at zero lower bound signals deflationary pressure and monetary policy impotence, causing equity risk premiums to widen and producing negative 20-day returns as growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 when both conditions are simultaneously satisfied.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at rates below 2% annualized signals sub-trend economic expansion and weak aggregate demand, causing equity valuations to compress as terminal growth expectations are revised downward over the 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2012 GDPC1 at 13558.0 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: sub-trend growth or secular stagnation regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in sub-2% growth quarters exceed baseline returns by > 30 basis points (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerating above 750k annualized units combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals strengthening consumer demand and housing cycle recovery, producing positive 5-day equity returns through improved forward earnings visibility and reduced recession risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 750k annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
regime_hint: housing-led demand recovery with consumer spending acceleration
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 5-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 when both conditions are met simultaneously over any 2-year rolling window.
